# ruff

Modern, ultra-fast linter that implements checks from Flake8 and some other popular plugins.

lintquarto supports two ruff-based code rewriting commands:

* `lintquarto -f ruff-check-fix` runs `ruff check --fix`.
- `lintquarto -f ruff-format` runs `ruff format`

For read-only linting, see the [ruff linting page](ruff.qmd).

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Import required packages
from IPython.display import Markdown, display
from pathlib import Path
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

# Add parent directory to sys.path
sys.path.insert(0, "..")

# Import the helper functions
from helpers import generate_html, print_quarto
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_html(
    pypi_url="https://pypi.org/project/ruff/",
    github_url="https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff",
    conda_url="https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/ruff"
)
```

## Example Quarto `.qmd` file:

{{< downloadthis examples/general_example.qmd dname="general_example" label="Download the example" type="light" >}}

:::{.pale-grey}
```{python}
#| echo: false
print_quarto("examples/general_example.qmd")
```
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
def run_on_temp_qmd(source, command):
    source = Path(source)
    tmp_dir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp())
    tmp_qmd = tmp_dir / source.name
    shutil.copy(source, tmp_qmd)

    result = subprocess.run(
        [
            sys.executable,
            "-m",
            "lintquarto",
            "-f",
            command,
            "-p",
            str(tmp_qmd),
        ],
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
        check=False,
    )


    return {
        "path": tmp_qmd,
        "stdout": result.stdout,
        "stderr": result.stderr,
        "returncode": result.returncode,
        "content": tmp_qmd.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    }
```

## `ruff-format`

Use this command to format Python code blocks in a Quarto document.

```{.bash}
lintquarto -f ruff-format -p general_example.qmd
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
result = run_on_temp_qmd("examples/general_example.qmd", "ruff-format")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
log = result["stdout"].rstrip("\n")
display(Markdown(f"````text\n{log}\n````"))
```

New file:

:::{.pale-grey}
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
log = result["content"].rstrip("\n")
display(Markdown(f"````text\n{log}\n````"))
```
:::

## `ruff-check-fix`

Use this command to automatically apply Ruff's available fixes for supported lint violations.

```{.bash}
lintquarto -f ruff-check-fix -p general_example.qmd
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
result = run_on_temp_qmd("examples/general_example.qmd", "ruff-check-fix")
```

New file:

:::{.pale-grey}
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
log = result["content"].rstrip("\n")
display(Markdown(f"````text\n{log}\n````"))
```
:::

It will also print to console with messages about the remaining issues that couldn't be automatically fixed.
